title: Document execution API contract
description: Acceptance: /openapi.json includes request/response details for campaign creation, provider events, and pilot execution so an API evaluator can discover the product workflow programmatically. Validation: server test asserts the execution path has a requestBody and 200 response description.
close_reason: Expanded /openapi.json with request/response details for campaign creation, provider events, and pilot execution; server test asserts execution path requestBody and 200 proof response description.

title: Add Docker runtime smoke proof
description: Add an optional smoke:docker gate that builds the Docker image, runs it with production-style API/webhook secrets and a temp data volume, checks health/openapi, confirms protected routes reject unauthenticated calls, and fetches the launch packet with API key auth. Document it as deployability proof without making live Instagram claims.
close_reason: Added and verified npm run smoke:docker, documented Docker runtime proof, refreshed status MMDX, and preserved external live-pilot/MMDX-auth blockers.

title: Add managed service deploy smoke path
description: Acceptance: the public repo has a deployment-oriented service smoke command that starts the built API process with an isolated store, verifies /health and /openapi.json, creates/approves/executes a managed-provider contract campaign, checks readiness/proof metrics, then shuts down cleanly. Docs describe the command and container/runtime env. Validation: npm run build && npm run smoke:service plus existing tests/typecheck pass; README/domain/status MMDX reflect deployment hardening as green or gap-covered.
close_reason: Added validated runtime config, JSON-store health checks, service smoke command, Docker packaging, docs/runbook/status updates, and verified build/smoke/test/typecheck/MMDX.

title: Persist approval workbench API
description: Acceptance: campaigns can create/get a persistent approval workbench over creator targets and first-touch copy; API can approve/reject creators and approve copy before execution; executions without inline approval overrides use stored approval state when present; JSON store persists the approval workbench across restarts. Validation: server tests cover create approval -> approve one/reject one -> execute only approved target -> fetch persisted workbench, and store tests cover approval persistence.
close_reason: Persisted approval workbenches, added approval workbench and decision API routes, made executions reuse stored approvals, updated docs/status, and validated through tests plus live API smoke.

title: Make evaluator source and domain docs citation-grade
description: Outcome: evaluator-facing docs no longer require readers to trust uncited bounty/build-vs-clone/domain assumptions. Acceptance: docs capture the bounty source URL and access limits, external Instagram/Messaging/product-shape facts with URLs and retrieval dates, BUILD_VS_CLONE Source Notes link to source evidence, DOMAIN_PLAN separates delivered slices from remaining blockers, status MMDX reflects source/domain drift as resolved, and remaining external blockers stay explicit. Validation: docs/source searches, MMDX preflight, br ready/blocked checks, git diff --check.
close_reason: Added citation-grade source evidence, clarified bounty access limits, split delivered domain slices from live proof blockers, refreshed README/build-vs-clone/status MMDX, and validated with MMDX preflight, consistency search, br ready/blocked, and git diff --check.

title: Enforce approval readiness before executions
description: Acceptance: POST /campaigns/:id/executions rejects unapproved campaigns with a clear 409 instead of synthesizing approvals when readiness still requires approval. Stored approval workbenches and explicit inline approvals continue to work. Regression tests cover readiness needs_approval -> execution 409, stored approval execution, and inline approval execution. Validation: targeted server/readiness/manual rehearsal tests plus full test/typecheck pass.
close_reason: Execution now rejects campaigns that fail readiness approval gates, preserves stored and explicit inline approval paths, and regression tests cover no-proof creation before approval.

title: Harden pilot OpenAPI contract
description: Acceptance: /openapi.json documents the public pilot contract closely enough for a Graphed operator to run the credential-free manual pilot flow: path params, Idempotency-Key, campaign template/metadata/settings/sender accounts/follow-ups, health/webhook preview, manual evidence event cases, and simulated-webhook wording. Validation: OpenAPI tests fail if templated paths omit parameters, campaign runtime fields disappear, or manual evidence requirements drift.
close_reason: Hardened /openapi.json with path params, idempotency headers, runtime campaign fields, manual evidence cases, health/webhook preview, docs, tests, and live smoke.

title: Define pilot acceptance contract
description: Acceptance: docs define ICP, offer, outreach volume, success thresholds, non-goals, compliance constraints, renewal criteria, and pilot proof pack. Validation: docs/BOUNTY_REQUIREMENTS.md and docs/VISION.md include the contract.
close_reason: Completed in first scaffold: campaign API/domain tests pass; README/VISION/bounty/domain/pilot docs are present; status MMDX preflight passed.

title: Harden launch authorization evidence gate
description: Require live manual/provider launch authorization to be fresh and evidence-backed so pilot proof packets cannot rely on stale or unauditable approval references. Acceptance: schema/runtime validation rejects stale or missing authorization evidence for manual/provider executions, docs describe the requirement, and targeted tests plus proof gate pass.
close_reason: Implemented fresh evidence-backed launch authorization gate with schema/runtime validation, docs/examples/status updates, and proof:bounty-local verification.

title: Refresh bounty local proof dossier
description: Outcome: evaluator-facing proof docs reflect current HEAD and current validation output after the launch-packet/handoff/authorization slices. Acceptance: docs/proof/delivery-path-dry-run.md records the current commit/date/tool versions, summarizes npm test/typecheck/build/smoke/manual-pilot results, includes current service-smoke/manual-rehearsal machine evidence, and explicitly distinguishes local proof from live Instagram/Graphed pilot evidence. README links the proof dossier from the status/documentation surface. Validation: npm test, npm run typecheck, npm run build, npm run smoke:service, npm run demo:manual-pilot, MMDX preflight, git diff --check.
close_reason: Refreshed the bounty local proof dossier, README link, marketing claim, and project-status MMDX with current validation evidence. Verified npm test, typecheck, build, smoke:service, demo:manual-pilot, demo:pilot, MMDX preflight, git diff --check, and Beads cycle check.

title: Implement signed outgoing webhooks with retries
description: Acceptance: campaign and target events deliver to webhookUrl with HMAC signatures, retry/backoff, dead-letter state, and replay. Validation: tests cover signing, retry, failure, and replay.
close_reason: Implemented outgoing signed webhook payload builders, injected dispatcher retries/backoff, dead-letter state, and replay with focused Vitest coverage.

title: Expose pilot execution API workflow
description: Acceptance: API exposes a safe pilot execution endpoint that loads a campaign, builds approvals from request data, routes through an injected mock/manual-safe adapter configuration, records campaign events, emits simulated signed webhook delivery records, and returns proof-pack metrics and markdown. Validation: server tests cover create campaign -> execute -> proof response and safety posture that mock/manual execution does not claim real Instagram delivery.
close_reason: Exposed POST /campaigns/:id/executions for safe mock/manual pilot execution, approval generation, adapter routing, simulated signed webhook records, and proof-pack response; server tests cover mock proof and manual non-live posture.

title: Guard outbound webhook destinations
description: Outcome: network-exposed deployments cannot be abused to call localhost, private networks, or arbitrary webhook hosts. Acceptance: runtime config reads allowed webhook hosts, server validates campaign webhookUrl before storing/dispatching, localhost/private IP destinations are rejected by default, explicit allowlist permits intended callback hosts, tests cover blocked/allowed/no-config paths, docs/status describe the deployment gate.
close_reason: Webhook destination guard implemented with tests/docs

title: Prove selected manual path through service smoke
description: Outcome: the compiled API service smoke proves the selected manual pilot path, not only the managed-provider contract. Acceptance: npm run smoke:service starts dist/index.js with API key auth and JSON store, then runs profile-object manual campaign creation, approval/claim, manual execution, manual queue, sent/replied/restricted evidence with occurredAt timestamps, sender-risk reconciliation, simulated webhook records, proof metrics, and evidence_ready readiness. Validation: npm run smoke:service, npm test, npm run typecheck, MMDX preflight, git diff --check.
close_reason: npm run smoke:service now builds first, starts dist/index.js with API key auth and JSON store, then proves both managed-provider and selected manual paths over HTTP including manual queue, timestamped evidence, sender-risk reconciliation, webhook metrics, proof metrics, and evidence_ready readiness. Verified smoke:service, npm test, typecheck, MMDX preflight, git diff --check, and Beads cycle check.

title: Ship campaign API control-plane MVP
description: Acceptance: POST /campaigns accepts bounty-shaped payload; targets normalize; duplicates skip; safe per-sender schedule is returned; provider events update delivery/reply summary; npm test/typecheck/build pass.
close_reason: Completed in first scaffold: campaign API/domain tests pass; README/VISION/bounty/domain/pilot docs are present; status MMDX preflight passed.

title: Refresh latest execution proof after late provider events
description: Outcome: provider events recorded after an execution cannot leave the campaign-level proof export or follow-up plan stale. Acceptance: POST /campaigns/:id/events updates or refreshes the latest execution proof pack when it records a new delivery/reply/failure event for a target in the latest execution; GET /campaigns/:id/proof-pack reflects late replies/failures; GET /campaigns/:id/follow-ups suppresses replied, failed, or restricted targets after late provider events; tests cover late reply suppressing follow-up and proof metrics updating from 0 replies to 1. Validation: targeted server/followUp tests, npm test, typecheck, build, smoke:service, MMDX preflight, git diff --check.
close_reason: Implemented late provider event proof refresh with follow-up suppression; verified targeted tests, npm test, typecheck, build, smoke:service, MMDX preflight, diff check, and dependency cycles.

title: Update marketing claim contract for Docker smoke
description: After adding smoke:docker, update the public marketing/claim contract so current local proof evidence includes Docker deployability proof and remains consistent with the bounty acceptance matrix and proof dossier.
close_reason: Updated marketing claim contract so current local proof dossier evidence includes Docker smoke and MMDX dry-run proof.

title: Expose pilot handoff packet
description: Outcome: a Graphed evaluator or operator can fetch one campaign-level handoff packet that turns readiness gates into exact external inputs, API actions, evidence requirements, callback/proof URLs, stop conditions, and current launch state. Acceptance: GET /campaigns/:id/pilot-handoff returns readiness, missing external inputs, next API actions for approval/execution/manual evidence/proof review, sender and creator input contracts, proof criteria, and source URLs; OpenAPI documents the route; README/runbook/status mention it as the live-pilot handoff surface; tests cover pre-approval missing inputs and evidence-ready empty-input proof review. Validation: targeted server tests, npm test, typecheck, build, smoke:service, MMDX preflight, git diff --check.
close_reason: Implemented pilot handoff route with OpenAPI, docs/status updates, proof refresh, and regression coverage; verified tests, typecheck, build, service smoke, manual demo, MMDX preflight, and diff check.

title: Persist managed sender inventory
description: Acceptance: API can register/list sender accounts, inspect sender inventory health, append risk events that change sender state, and create campaigns from stored sender inventory when inline senderAccounts are absent. Validation: domain/server/store/OpenAPI tests cover sender upsert, health blockers, JSON persistence, risk event updates, and campaign scheduling using stored healthy or blocked senders; docs/status describe sender operations as a managed-product surface.
close_reason: Implemented persistent managed sender inventory with sender APIs, risk-event health updates, stored-inventory campaign scheduling, readiness/execution rechecks, tests, docs, and status MMDX.

title: Add live pilot intake kit
description: Acceptance: add executable pilot intake templates and validation that match the real campaign, sender, authorization, and webhook contracts so Graphed/operator can supply live pilot inputs without guessing. Update bounty/runbook/status docs and validate locally.
close_reason: Added live pilot intake examples, schema-backed validator, proof command integration, docs/status updates, and verified npm run proof:bounty-local.

title: Follow-up scheduler used lexical sort on sent timestamps
description: latestSentAt() in src/domain/followUp.ts sorted delivery 'sent' events by lexical string order to pick the most recent send. For occurredAt values carrying a non-Z timezone offset (e.g. read back from the persisted JSON store, which is not re-validated against the Zod z.string().datetime() Z-only schema), the lexical order disagrees with chronological order, so the wrong send was chosen as the base for follow-up dueAt timing. Fixed to compare by parsed instant (Date.parse) with a NaN guard. Added a load-bearing test in tests/followUp.test.ts that fails before and passes after.
close_reason: Fixed in followUp.ts: sort sent events by parsed instant instead of lexical string order; covered by new timezone-offset test

title: Add approval and operator workbench flow
description: Acceptance: creators and copy can be approved/rejected before send; operator can claim work, mark sent/skipped/blocked, attach evidence, and log incidents. Validation: API or CLI tests cover approvals and audit trail.
close_reason: Implemented approval/workbench domain module with focused Vitest coverage

title: Add creator profile provenance intake
description: Outcome: pilot campaigns can preserve vetted creator profile evidence instead of only opaque handle strings. Acceptance: POST /campaigns accepts target profile objects with source and fit rationale while preserving string targets; CampaignTarget stores profile provenance; optional requireTargetProvenance blocks unvetted targets; readiness and proof metrics expose vetted target counts; OpenAPI, README/runbook/domain/status docs describe the pilot gate; tests cover string compatibility, profile storage, required provenance blocking, readiness, proof metrics, and API response shape.
close_reason: Implemented creator profile provenance intake, strict target provenance gating, readiness/proof vetted target metrics, OpenAPI/docs/status updates, and regression coverage. Verified with affected Vitest suite, npm test, typecheck, build, diff check, MMDX preflight, and service smoke.

title: Add manual evidence recording API
description: Acceptance: manual executions expose a POST endpoint to record operator evidence for sent/failed/restricted/replied outcomes; evidence validation reuses the manual adapter requirements; campaign status, webhook delivery records, stored execution attempts, reply assessments, and proof pack are refreshed after evidence; GET execution returns the updated proof. Validation: server tests cover manual execute -> record sent -> record replied -> fetch proof, plus missing evidence rejection.
close_reason: Added idempotent manual evidence API for manual executions, persisted refreshed proof packs, documented the route, and covered success/error paths in server tests.

title: Add opt-in Docker bounty proof mode
description: Expose Docker smoke as an opt-in extension of the local bounty proof path so evaluators can run the fast default proof without Docker or a fuller proof that includes container health/auth/launch-packet verification.
close_reason: Added proof:bounty-local:docker opt-in path, verified it end-to-end, and refreshed evaluator proof hashes/docs.

title: Deep research (GTM): how influencer-outreach and creator-DM tools are sold to agency and brand buyers
description:
**Why this matters for inschneidergram (marketing/GTM):** Inschneidergram is built to win a specific bounty (Graphed's managed Instagram outreach API) — but the broader market question is who *else* would buy a "programmatic Instagram creator outreach as infrastructure" product, how comparable influencer-CRM and creator-sourcing tools are positioned and priced, and what sales motion (product-led, agency-direct, brand-direct, marketplace-listed) actually works for tools in this category. Understanding the buyer landscape and proven sales patterns for creator-outreach tooling is the critical input for any post-bounty commercialization or positioning decision.

**Round-1 deep-research already covers:** Compliant Instagram cold-DM delivery paths — technical and policy landscape for sending at scale.

---

## Deep research prompt (marketing / GTM / SEO / content)

**Role + mission:** You are a GTM researcher specializing in the influencer-marketing technology stack — specifically how creator-outreach, influencer-CRM, and creator-sourcing tools are positioned, priced, and sold to agency and brand buyers.

**Context:**

Inschneidergram is an API-first control plane for managed Instagram creator outreach. It provides campaign management, provenance-backed creator intake, duplicate suppression, sender-limit scheduling, delivery/reply event logging, and operator-controlled manual or managed-provider execution. The current bounty target is Graphed — an influencer-marketing agency that needs a programmatic, policy-aware Instagram DM outreach product that their agents can call like infrastructure.

The post-bounty question is: who else needs this, and how is this category actually sold? The influencer-marketing technology market includes: creator-discovery/search tools (AspireIQ, Grin, Upfluence, Klear, Heepsy), full-platform influencer-CRM tools (Grin, CreatorIQ, Mavrck, Traackr), outreach-automation tools (Pitchbox positioned for PR/influencer outreach, Hunter.io + email sequences adapted for creator outreach), and boutique Instagram-specific DM tools (many of which operate in grey-market territory). The buyer personas break into three main segments: (a) performance marketing agencies and influencer-marketing agencies with recurring client campaigns; (b) direct brands with in-house influencer programs; and (c) marketplaces and creator-economy platforms that need creator-communication infrastructure.

The specific positioning gap inschneidergram could occupy: API-first, compliance-conscious, operator-controlled creator outreach — not a self-serve automation tool, not a full discovery platform, but the delivery and campaign-management layer that agencies and technical marketers call programmatically. This is a narrower wedge than the "all-in-one influencer platform" plays, but it may be the exact infrastructure layer that agencies who have already solved discovery (they have the lists) are missing.

The research gap is: what does the buyer journey, pricing model, and sales motion actually look like for influencer-outreach and creator-communication tools sold to agency and brand buyers — and is the "API-first infrastructure" wedge an under-served niche or already crowded?

**Research questions:**

1. Who are the documented buyers of influencer-outreach and creator-CRM tools at the agency and brand level — and what does the buying process look like? Specifically: what budget line owns influencer-tech spend (performance marketing, social, brand partnerships), what is the typical ACV/ARR range for tools in this category, what is the typical sales cycle length, and who is the champion vs. economic buyer in the purchase? Map at least 5 tools (Grin, CreatorIQ, Upfluence, Mavrck, AspireIQ, Traackr, or comparable) with their documented pricing, ICP, and sales motion.

2. Is there a documented market for "API-first / programmatic" creator-outreach infrastructure sold to technical agency buyers or creator-economy platform builders — separate from the full-suite influencer-platform plays? Specifically: are there tools, platforms, or API products that position themselves as the outreach/communication layer for agencies or platforms that have already solved creator discovery? What are their pricing models, documented customer types, and GTM approach?

3. What are the dominant acquisition channels for influencer-marketing technology tools targeting agency buyers? Map the specific channels that work: G2/Capterra category presence, LinkedIn sponsored content, influencer-marketing conference sponsorships (Influencer Marketing World, ANA, Digiday), agency partnership programs, product-hunt launches, content SEO for queries like "best influencer outreach tool" or "Instagram DM automation for agencies." For each channel: evidence of use by at least 2 tools, estimated reach/cost/conversion, and whether it works for early-stage vs. established players.

4. What are the specific objections and trust-blockers that agency/brand buyers cite when evaluating Instagram-specific outreach tools — particularly around account risk, Meta policy compliance, and data provenance? How do the leading tools handle these objections in their marketing copy, sales collateral, and product design? What evidence format (compliance documentation, SOC2, case studies, pilot programs) do buyers require before committing budget?

**Output format:**

Deliver a structured markdown report with these sections:

- **Section 1 — Buyer and pricing map:** N-entity table — tool name, ICP (agency/brand/platform), pricing model, ACV range, sales motion (PLG/sales-assisted/enterprise), documented customer types, source.
- **Section 2 — API/programmatic niche assessment:** Structured analysis of whether the "API-first outreach infrastructure" wedge is under-served or crowded — with named competitors, their positioning language, and honest gap analysis.
- **Section 3 — Agency acquisition channel audit:** N-entity table — channel, tools known to use it, estimated reach/cost, effectiveness evidence, early-stage vs. established fit.
- **Section 4 — Objection and trust-blocker map:** N-entity table — objection type, how leading tools address it in marketing/sales, what evidence format buyers require, confidence in claim.
- **Section 5 — Prioritized GTM recommendation for inschneidergram post-bounty:** Top 3 positioning and acquisition moves in priority order with rationale and confidence flag.

**Hard constraints:**

- Authoritative/primary sources only: G2/Capterra reviews with verifiable timestamps, tool pricing pages, published case studies, conference sponsor lists, verified LinkedIn ads library examples, industry analyst reports (Forrester, Influencer Marketing Hub annual benchmarks). Do not cite generic "best influencer marketing tools" SEO listicles without verifiable primary data behind the claims.
- All ACV/pricing/reach figures must be traceable or marked "not found / inferred."
- No fabricated metrics or invented customer examples.
- Scope only: influencer-outreach, creator-CRM, and creator-communication infrastructure tools — not general email outreach tools or social media management platforms unless they have a documented influencer-specific product line.
- Confidence flags required on all market-sizing and conversion claims.

**Report-back criteria:**

1. At least 5 influencer-tech tools mapped with pricing, ICP, and sales motion evidence.
2. API/programmatic niche assessed with named competitors or honest "no direct competitor found."
3. At least 4 agency acquisition channels analyzed with effectiveness evidence.
4. Objection/trust-blocker map with at least 4 documented objection types and how they are handled.
5. A prioritized top-3 post-bounty GTM recommendation with honest confidence flags.
close_reason: Completed source-backed GTM buyer-market research report at docs/research/2026-06-01-influencer-crm-buyer-market.md

title: Deep research: Instagram creator outreach delivery landscape — compliant cold-DM paths and managed provider options
description:
**Why this matters for inschneidergram:** inschneidergram has a complete API control plane but zero live delivery: the repo explicitly notes "real Instagram delivery: not implemented" and the whole bounty depends on connecting to a real delivery operation. The critical external unknown is which delivery paths — official Meta API, third-party managed providers, operator-run accounts — are actually viable for cold creator outreach in 2025-2026, what their compliance posture and account-risk profile look like, and what pricing/operational model each demands. Choosing the wrong path wastes the pilot; choosing the right one closes the bounty.

---

## Deep research prompt

You are a platform-policy and API-landscape analyst. Your sole task is to assess the available delivery paths for programmatic Instagram creator outreach (cold direct messages to a list of creators/influencers) as of 2025-2026, with explicit attention to compliance posture, account risk, and operational requirements. You do NOT advise on content strategy, audience growth, or general social media marketing. You do NOT produce legal advice.

### Context

A product called Inschneidergram is building a managed Instagram creator outreach control plane: an API-first system that accepts a creator list, campaign settings, and message copy, then orchestrates delivery with approval gates, sender-limit scheduling, duplicate prevention, and proof-pack generation. The critical gap is the delivery layer itself. The Meta/Instagram official API does not offer a documented blanket permission for arbitrary cold outbound DMs to any profile; the official messaging surface is inbox/reply-focused for professional accounts, with 24-hour messaging windows and comment-triggered patterns. Yet the market for programmatic creator outreach clearly exists (ManyChat, Unipile, Lyncly, Waveloop, and similar products operate in it). The question is: what are the actual viable delivery paths, their compliance profiles, their account-risk profiles, and their operational/pricing requirements for a managed product that wants to run small, auditable pilot campaigns for client growth teams? This research informs the delivery-path decision that determines whether the bounty pilot can be completed.

### Research questions

1. **Official Meta/Instagram API — cold outreach scope.** What does the current Meta developer documentation (as of 2025) actually permit for outbound DMs to creators who have not messaged first? Specifically: what permissions (Standard vs. Advanced Access, human-agent flag) govern this, what messaging windows apply, and is there any documented path for cold outreach to a list of profiles who have not initiated contact?

2. **Third-party managed delivery providers.** Which providers (e.g., Unipile, Phantombuster, ManyChat, Lyncly, Waveloop, DMFlow, or others) currently offer a managed or API-accessible Instagram DM delivery layer? For each: what is their stated compliance posture (official API vs. unofficial automation), pricing model, account-risk language, and known restrictions on cold outreach use cases?

3. **Account risk profile by delivery path.** What does public evidence (Meta enforcement actions, user reports, community forum data) say about the account-restriction and ban-rate experience for: (a) official API sending; (b) third-party managed API providers; (c) unofficial browser automation? What warm-up, rate-limit, and sender-diversity practices are documented as reducing risk?

4. **Operator-run account model.** For products that use operator-owned Instagram accounts as senders (rather than client accounts): what are the documented risks, legal exposure, and platform-policy implications? Is there public evidence of this model being used at commercial scale?

5. **Platform policy trajectory.** Is there public evidence (Meta developer blog posts, policy announcements, enforcement trends, industry commentary) suggesting that Instagram's API or policy posture toward programmatic creator outreach is tightening, loosening, or stable as of 2025-2026?

6. **Minimum viable pilot requirements.** Based on publicly documented provider contracts and API documentation, what are the minimum operational requirements (account type, permission tier, warm-up period, daily limit, message volume) for running a low-volume, auditable creator-outreach pilot (e.g., 20-50 creators) without significant account-restriction risk?

### Output format

Produce a structured report with the following sections:

**1. Official Instagram API — Cold Outreach Scope**
- Finding summary (1 paragraph)
- Specific permissions, windows, and restrictions documented with source URLs
- Explicit verdict: does the official API support cold outreach to an arbitrary creator list? (yes / no / conditional — with conditions named)

**2. Third-Party Managed Delivery Providers**
- Table: provider name | API/managed | stated compliance posture | cold outreach support (yes/no/unclear) | pricing model | account-risk language | last verified date | source URL
- At least 5 providers assessed

**3. Account Risk Evidence**
- For each delivery path: documented restriction/ban rates or incident reports (source), documented risk-reduction practices (source), confidence level (high/medium/low/inferred)

**4. Operator-Run Account Model**
- Documented examples or absence of evidence; risk and policy implications; explicit [NOT FOUND] if no public evidence

**5. Platform Policy Trajectory**
- 3–5 bullet summary of policy signals with source and date; direction assessment (tightening/stable/loosening)

**6. Minimum Viable Pilot Requirements**
- Bulleted list of documented minimum requirements for a low-risk 20-50 creator pilot, sourced to provider docs or Meta documentation
- Flag any requirement that is undocumented or inferred

**7. Delivery Path Decision Matrix**
- Table: delivery path | compliance posture | account risk level | operational complexity | cost range | verdict (viable/conditional/avoid)
- 3–5 bullet recommendation for a managed product that needs an auditable pilot path

### Hard constraints

- Use only primary sources: Meta official developer documentation, provider public pricing pages and terms of service, Meta newsroom/policy announcements, credible industry research (not blog posts), or direct platform enforcement notices. Do not cite aggregator roundups without tracing each claim to a primary source.
- Mark any claim not traceable to a source as [NOT FOUND] or [INFERRED].
- Do not fabricate API permissions, provider pricing, restriction rates, or citations.
- Clearly distinguish between official Meta API capability and third-party unofficial automation.
- Do not conflate comment-triggered DMs (private replies) with cold outreach to a creator list.
- Avoid: vendor marketing copy repeated as compliance evidence; speculation about unreleased Meta API features; generic "influencer marketing is growing" filler.
- Flag genuine uncertainty, especially where provider terms use vague compliance language.

### Completion criteria

Report is complete when it delivers:
1. A clear finding on whether the official Meta API supports cold outreach to an arbitrary creator list, with specific permission and window evidence.
2. A provider table covering at least 5 named third-party delivery options with compliance posture and cold-outreach support assessment.
3. Account risk evidence for at least 3 delivery paths with confidence ratings.
4. A delivery path decision matrix with at least 4 paths evaluated.
5. A 3–5 bullet recommendation identifying the most viable path for a managed, auditable 20-50 creator pilot.
notes: Oracle execute attempted 2026-06-01. Prompt written to /tmp/inschneidergram-instagram-delivery-deep-research-2026-06-01.md. Route guard passed; Oracle browser run launched with pre-submit Deep Research toggle hook on Chrome 127.0.0.1:9222. Oracle session slug: inschneide-instagram-delivery; reattach: oracle session inschneide-instagram-delivery. Candidate ChatGPT URL: https://chatgpt.com/c/6a1dd561-83dc-83e8-92e6-4e8b754e6329. CLI completed after ~20m with only 'Pro thinking' / no dossier. Bundled watcher then ran after the 30m no-poll window: DEEP_RESEARCH_OUTPUT=/tmp/inschneidergram-instagram-delivery-oracle-output-2026-06-01.md ORACLE_CHATGPT_URL_MATCH=6a1dd561-83dc-83e8-92e6-4e8b754e6329 node /Users/b/repos/opensource/skills/deep-research-prompt/assets/scripts/await-deep-research.mjs --no-initial-delay. Watcher polled every 15m and timed out after ~2h with no assistant/research output visible, so no output file was produced. Blocker: ChatGPT/Deep Research conversation did not expose/produce assistant output for capture despite successful Oracle launch path; do not claim the research completed.

title: Document delivery-path decision and provider landscape for bounty submission
description: Outcome: evaluator-facing decision record (docs/DELIVERY_PATH_DECISION.md) that proves, via the verified 2026-05-31 5-category provider survey, that no compliant managed cold-DM provider exists, and justifies the operator-run manual managed path as the only managed + cold + honest-per-message-status option. Referenced from BOUNTY_SUBMISSION.md so evaluators reach it. Evidence: docs/DELIVERY_PATH_DECISION.md present; BOUNTY_SUBMISSION.md 'What To Inspect' table + Pilot Ask link it; sources cited with adversarial-verification caveats. Failure avoided: shipping a defensive-only submission that reads as 'we could not do cold DMs' instead of 'we surveyed the market and chose the honest managed path' — losing the domain-depth differentiator.
notes: Done in tree 2026-05-31: docs/DELIVERY_PATH_DECISION.md written and linked from BOUNTY_SUBMISSION.md (What To Inspect row + Pilot Ask item 3). Markdown-only change; no code touched, test suite stays green from prior tick. Completion gate: commit-blocked — changes uncommitted pending operator authorization to commit.

title: Generate pilot proof dashboard and report
description: Acceptance: report shows sourced, approved, contacted, delivered, replied, interested, opt-outs, complaints, duplicate skips, sender health, incidents, and renewal recommendation. Validation: sample pilot fixture generates the proof pack.
close_reason: Implemented proof-pack generator with sample pilot fixture covering sourced, approved, contacted, sent, delivered, replied, interested, opt-outs, complaints, duplicate skips, sender health, incidents, webhook outcomes, and renewal recommendation.

title: Model sender account operations and health
description: Acceptance: sender inventory tracks limits, cooldowns, lockouts, reconnect-required state, warm-up notes, and risk events. Validation: scheduler refuses unhealthy senders and reports sender health in campaign status.
close_reason: Implemented sender account health model with limits, cooldowns, lockouts, reconnect-required state, warm-up notes, risk events, scheduler refusal, and API senderHealth reporting; tests cover domain and API behavior.

title: Expose operator workbench state API
description: Acceptance: approval workbench candidates can be claimed by an operator and marked skipped or blocked through persisted API routes; execution only creates send intents for approved candidates whose work state is queued or claimed, never skipped/blocked/sent; API responses preserve audit/evidence. Validation: server tests cover claim -> skip/block persistence and execution exclusion, execution domain test guards skipped/blocked candidates, OpenAPI/docs/status are updated.
close_reason: Exposed persisted operator claim and skip/block workbench routes, made execution ignore non-actionable approved candidates, updated docs/status, and validated with tests plus live API smoke.

title: Add bounty acceptance evidence matrix
description: Create an evaluator-facing matrix that maps the interpreted Graphed bounty requirements to current repo evidence, non-claims, proof commands, and external blockers. Link it from the submission packet and project status surface. Validate docs/MMDX status without needing private credentials.
close_reason: Added public bounty acceptance evidence matrix, linked it from submission/README/status MMDX, and refreshed MMDX preflight/dry-run evidence.

title: Dispatch manual evidence webhooks through runtime sender
description: Outcome: manual evidence cannot be counted as delivered unless a real runtime webhook sender, or an explicitly simulated local rehearsal sender, handled the callback. Acceptance: POST /campaigns/:id/executions/:executionId/manual-events defaults to the runtime guarded webhook dispatcher; local demo/test simulation requires simulateWebhookDelivery=true; proof metrics count runtime delivered/dead-lettered callbacks; OpenAPI/docs describe the distinction; tests cover runtime sender invocation and explicit simulation. Validation: targeted server/manual rehearsal tests, npm test, typecheck, build, smoke:service, demo:manual-pilot, MMDX preflight, git diff --check.
close_reason: Manual evidence now dispatches through the runtime guarded webhook sender by default; local simulated callback delivery requires simulateWebhookDelivery=true. Added runtime sender regression coverage, updated OpenAPI/docs/status, and kept local rehearsal explicit. Verified with targeted/manual tests, npm test, typecheck, build, service smoke, demo, MMDX preflight, and git diff --check.

title: Add one-command manual pilot rehearsal
description: Acceptance: npm script runs a credential-free manual pilot rehearsal that exercises campaign creation, approvals, readiness, manual execution, sent/replied evidence, proof-pack renewal output, and OpenAPI contract availability. Validation: focused script test or smoke asserts readiness moves from ready_to_execute/awaiting_manual_evidence to evidence_ready and proof metrics show contacted/interested reply; README/runbook/status docs describe the command.
close_reason: Added credential-free manual pilot rehearsal script and tests, documented npm run demo:manual-pilot, updated status MMDX, and validated full gates.

title: Win Graphed Instagram creator outreach bounty
description: Outcome: build a managed, API-first Instagram creator outreach product that can run a real Graphed pilot and produce delivery/reply proof. Evidence: public repo, tests, campaign API, managed delivery adapter, sender ops, pilot runbook, and proof report. Failure avoided: shipping only a fragile script or pitch deck.
notes: 2026-06-01 root reconciliation: parent epic is not implementation-ready. All local/build/proof children are closed except inschneidergram-j8b.3, which remains blocked on live Graphed pilot execution/proof authorization and should not be treated as ready code work. Reopen/claim this parent only after the live pilot proof blocker clears or a new local implementation child is created.

title: Select managed delivery path and sender accounts
description: Acceptance: choose the real pilot delivery path (managed provider or operator-run account pool), document account risk owner, sender limits, warm-up state, credential/reconnect process, and prove one low-risk test handoff without claiming official cold-DM compliance. Validation: pilot runbook records provider/account decision and a dry-run or test-send evidence artifact.
close_reason: Selected operator-run managed manual delivery for first pilot, documented private sender-account requirements and risk posture in docs/PILOT_RUNBOOK.md, and added dry-run evidence artifact without claiming live Instagram delivery.

title: Gate live pilot execution on launch authorization
description: Outcome: the API-visible launch path cannot run a real/manual/provider pilot without an explicit authorization record for the approved delivery path. Acceptance: campaign execution requests for manual or managed_provider delivery require a launch authorization reference with actor, delivery path, approved target limit, approvedAt timestamp, and evidence/reference; readiness includes a launch_authorization gate and does not present live-pilot execution as authorized while permission is missing; unauthorized live/manual/provider execution returns 409 while mock/local rehearsal remains available; proof export records the authorization reference; OpenAPI, docs, and tests cover missing and present authorization.
close_reason: Implemented launchAuthorization contract for manual and managed-provider execution, readiness gate, proof/export preservation, OpenAPI/docs/status updates, smoke/demo payloads, and regression coverage.

title: Expose pre-campaign pilot launch packet
description: Outcome: Graphed can ask the API what private inputs and exact payloads are required before a campaign exists, so the live-pilot blocker becomes an actionable launch packet instead of buried prose. Acceptance: expose a pre-campaign pilot launch packet in the API and OpenAPI that names required external inputs, creator-target schema, sender credential boundary, delivery-path options, launchAuthorization template, stop conditions, proof/report URLs, and validation commands; docs/status describe it; tests assert the route is protected by API key, returns the expected contracts, and is listed in OpenAPI. Validation: npm test, npm run typecheck, npm run build, MMDX preflight.
close_reason: Implemented pre-campaign pilot launch packet with protected API route, schema-grade OpenAPI response contract, README/runbook/spec/domain/status/proof updates, service-smoke coverage, and API tests; verified tests, typecheck, build, smoke, demos, MMDX, diff check, and dependency cycles.

title: Refresh evaluator proof provenance
description: Update the public proof dossier and source-evidence caveat so bounty evaluators can tie the local proof run to current HEAD and understand the Notion access limit.
close_reason: Refreshed proof dossier provenance to current HEAD and rechecked bounty source access caveat.

title: Prevent proof examples from expiring
description: Refresh bundled public example launch authorization windows at runtime for local proof and rehearsals while preserving strict freshness validation for private pilot files.
close_reason: Runtime-renewed bundled public proof authorization windows while preserving strict private authorization validation.

title: Require managed-provider outcome evidence
description: Enforce event-specific evidence fields for managed-provider outcomes so provider proof cannot be recorded from bare event types. Sent/replied events must carry message ids and evidence; replied must carry reply text; failed/restricted must carry reasons and evidence. Update OpenAPI/docs/tests.
close_reason: Enforced event-specific managed-provider evidence requirements, updated OpenAPI/docs/status, and reran full local bounty proof.

title: Refresh wiki-duel bounty readiness after hardening
description: Update the evaluator-facing wiki-duel readiness report after the example-authorization renewal and managed-provider evidence hardening patches, incorporating the final 95%+ verifier and fresh advocate/skeptic duel.
close_reason: Implemented canonical proof-packet export, refreshed proof/status/wiki-duel docs, reran local bounty proof, and reached post-hardening verifier confidence.

title: Add canonical proof-packet export
description: Implement a canonical redacted proof-packet export for the latest campaign execution so evaluators can replay the launch authorization, delivery attempts, webhook attempts, evidence references, follow-up plan, and source URLs with a deterministic SHA-256.
close_reason: Implemented canonical proof-packet export, refreshed proof/status/wiki-duel docs, reran local bounty proof, and reached post-hardening verifier confidence.

title: Stabilize manual rehearsal proof timeout
description: Raise the timeout only for the credential-free manual pilot rehearsal test so the one-command bounty proof does not fail when the intentional in-process proof path runs slightly over Vitest's default 5s budget.
close_reason: Raised only the credential-free manual pilot rehearsal test timeout and reran targeted test plus full local bounty proof successfully.

title: Wire campaign execution runner
description: Acceptance: campaign execution creates approved send intents from the approval workbench, routes them through the selected managed delivery adapter, records delivery/reply/restricted/failed events, dispatches outgoing webhooks, and feeds the proof-pack generator. Validation: integration test covers approval-to-delivery-to-webhook-to-proof flow using an injected provider adapter.
close_reason: Implemented execution runner that creates approved send intents, routes through injected delivery adapter, records sent/replied/restricted/failed campaign events, dispatches signed webhooks, and returns a proof pack; integration tests cover the full flow.

title: Run live Graphed pilot and publish proof
description: Acceptance: run a controlled Graphed pilot with a vetted creator list and selected managed delivery path, then publish a proof pack showing sourced, approved, contacted, delivered, replied, interested, opt-outs, complaints, duplicate skips, sender health, incidents, and renewal recommendation. Validation: final proof report is generated from real pilot records, not only sample fixtures.
notes: DELIVERY PATH DECIDED 2026-05-31: operator-run MANUAL managed (deliveryPath=manual, accountRiskOwner=operator). Verified deep-research found NO compliant managed cold-DM provider exists; official IG Messaging API structurally blocks cold outbound (24h customer-initiated window only); all cold-capable vendors (AutoReacher, Apify, Unipile, instagrapi) are gray-market ToS violations with ban risk. Human sends cold DMs from warmed accounts via the existing manual-evidence queue = only managed+cold path with honest per-message status. Still blocked on 4 private inputs: vetted creator list (source+fitReason per target), approved first-touch/follow-up copy, sender account access, manual launchAuthorization + HTTPS callback. No code-side work remains.

title: Expose manual delivery operator queue
description: Acceptance: API can return pending and completed manual delivery work for an execution without exposing raw internal execution records. Response includes execution/campaign ids, adapter posture, per-target manual queue entries with intent id, target, sender account id, message, scheduled time, evidence status, required evidence fields, existing terminal event if any, and proof-pack/manual evidence summary. Validation: server/OpenAPI/manual rehearsal tests cover empty queue, pending manual intents, sent/replied/restricted/failed completion, idempotent evidence preserving queue state, and docs/status describe the operator queue as the managed manual pilot work surface.
close_reason: Implemented operator manual delivery queue with global and execution-scoped read models, OpenAPI docs, rehearsal proof, tests, docs, and status MMDX.

title: Make manual evidence updates atomic
description: Acceptance: manual evidence recording updates campaign state and execution proof through one store-level mutation so concurrent operator evidence cannot lose events. Validation: JSON store/server tests cover concurrent manual evidence writes for distinct manual attempts, idempotent replay still returns existing event without duplicate webhook delivery, and docs/status note the queue/evidence path is safe for small multi-operator pilot use.
close_reason: Implemented store-level atomic campaign/execution mutation for manual evidence, routed manual-events through it, added JSON-store concurrency coverage, and updated docs/status.

title: Expose managed provider execution adapter
description: Acceptance: POST /campaigns/:id/executions accepts adapter.kind=managed_provider with explicit provider delivery outcomes, routes approved send intents through the domain managed provider adapter, records sent/replied/failed/restricted events into campaign status/proof/webhooks, and exposes a risk posture that clearly does not claim official cold-DM compliance. Validation: server/OpenAPI/domain integration tests cover provider sent/replied/restricted outcomes, proof-pack metrics, readiness after provider execution, webhook records, invalid provider event requests, and docs/status describe this as a provider contract surface rather than live Instagram delivery.
close_reason: Implemented managed-provider execution adapter surface with explicit provider outcomes, validation, proof/readiness/webhook coverage, docs/status updates, and full validation.

title: Mint public Buildooor MMDX status link
description: Acceptance: diagrams/inschneidergram-project-status.mmdx has shortLink.username=buildooor and shortLink.slug=mmdx-inschneidergram-project-status, and publish-link live-verifies the current source hash. Completed on 2026-05-30: Buildooor SPAPS auth refreshed; stable short link buildooor/mmdx-inschneidergram-project-status created; npm run status:mmdx:publish returned live_verification=OK for https://buildooor.com/mmdx/buildooor/mmdx-inschneidergram-project-status with source_sha256 b31bf0f5ea551b9d66bf771f1552d1a9114dc59a48b39bf4bd36193a09eef7ea.
notes: 2026-05-30 completion: npm run status:mmdx:login refreshed Buildooor SPAPS auth; npm run status:mmdx:list succeeded; missing stable short link was created through POST /api/app-links; npm run status:mmdx:publish then live-verified https://buildooor.com/mmdx/buildooor/mmdx-inschneidergram-project-status. After status text changed, publish was rerun and returned live_verification=OK with source_sha256 b31bf0f5ea551b9d66bf771f1552d1a9114dc59a48b39bf4bd36193a09eef7ea.
close_reason: Buildooor SPAPS auth refreshed, stable short link created, and npm run status:mmdx:publish live-verified buildooor/mmdx-inschneidergram-project-status with source_sha256 b31bf0f5ea551b9d66bf771f1552d1a9114dc59a48b39bf4bd36193a09eef7ea.

comments.author: b
comments.text: Final status-stack publish after docs/MMDX blocker text cleanup returned live_verification=OK with source_sha256 b31bf0f5ea551b9d66bf771f1552d1a9114dc59a48b39bf4bd36193a09eef7ea.

comments.author: b
comments.text: Reopened: Refresh close reason after final status-stack republish changed source hash.

title: Expose pilot follow-up plan
description: Outcome: Graphed/operator can see which approved contacted creators need scheduled follow-up work instead of only storing followUp settings as inert campaign config. Acceptance: derive follow-up items from campaign settings and latest execution evidence; do not plan follow-ups for replied, failed, restricted, duplicate, or policy-blocked targets; expose due/pending counts through a campaign API and latest proof export source URLs; include target provenance/sender/message/dueAt/sequence fields for operator use; OpenAPI, README/runbook/domain/status docs describe the pilot-safe follow-up surface; tests cover no-follow-up defaults, due/pending classification, reply/failure suppression, API response shape, and proof export linkage. Validation: targeted follow-up/server tests, npm test, typecheck, build, smoke:service, MMDX preflight, git diff --check.
close_reason: Implemented follow-up plan API, latest proof export linkage, OpenAPI/docs/status updates, and domain/server coverage. Verified with targeted Vitest, npm test, typecheck, build, service smoke, manual pilot demo, MMDX preflight, and git diff --check.

title: Reconcile manual restriction evidence into sender risk state
description: Outcome: a manual pilot restriction cannot leave the sender looking healthy or undercount sender risk in proof/readiness surfaces. Acceptance: recording manual evidence with type restricted appends a sender restriction risk event for the attempt sender when that sender exists in managed inventory; the affected sender becomes unavailable for later execution checks; proof metrics count the restriction as a sender warning; readiness reflects the current sender-health blocker after restriction evidence; docs/status note the stop-condition reconciliation; tests cover sender risk append, proof metric, readiness blocker, and subsequent execution 409. Validation: targeted server/sender/readiness tests, npm test, typecheck, build, smoke:service, MMDX preflight, git diff --check.
close_reason: Implemented manual restriction sender-risk reconciliation for managed inventory senders. Restricted manual evidence now appends a sender restriction event, moves the sender into cooldown, refreshes proof sender-warning metrics, and blocks readiness/subsequent execution. Verified with targeted server/sender/readiness tests, npm test, typecheck, build, service smoke, manual pilot demo, MMDX preflight, and git diff --check.

title: Add pilot intake API rehearsal
description: Acceptance: add a command that loads the validated pilot intake files, registers non-secret senders, creates the campaign, approves/claims targets, creates a manual execution with launch authorization, and prints handoff/readiness/manual queue URLs without recording fake live evidence. Include it in proof/docs/status.
close_reason: Added pilot intake API rehearsal, wired it into proof:bounty-local, updated handoff/status docs, and verified proof, mmdx preflight, and rehearsal.

title: Implement managed delivery adapter interface
description: Acceptance: outbox send intents can be claimed by mock, manual, or provider adapters; adapter reports sent/failed/restricted/replied events; no code claims official cold-DM compliance. Validation: adapter contract tests and a manual-provider pilot dry run.
close_reason: Implemented managed delivery adapter contract for mock, manual, and provider-backed paths with risk posture fixed to officialColdDmCompliance=not_claimed; tests cover sent, failed, restricted, replied, and manual evidence flows.

title: Expose latest campaign proof export
description: Acceptance: a campaign-level API route returns the latest proof pack with readiness, campaign summary, execution id, adapter posture, metrics, markdown, and next actions so buyers/operators do not need to inspect raw executions. It returns 404 when a campaign has no execution proof yet. OpenAPI, README/runbook/domain/status MMDX document the surface. Validation: targeted server/OpenAPI tests plus npm test, typecheck, build, smoke:service, and MMDX preflight pass.
close_reason: Added GET /campaigns/:id/proof-pack latest proof export with readiness context, source URLs, OpenAPI/docs/status updates, service smoke coverage, and tests for provider, manual, empty, and latest-execution paths.

title: Persist execution proof records
description: Acceptance: every pilot execution creates a durable execution record containing intents, adapter risk posture, delivery attempts, webhook delivery records, and proof pack; API can list/get execution records by campaign; JSON store persists records across process restarts. Validation: store tests cover execution persistence and server tests cover execute then list/get proof.
close_reason: Persisted execution proof records with list/get API coverage and JSON store persistence

title: Prove managed sender risk in manual rehearsal
description: Outcome: the public credential-free manual pilot demo proves the same managed sender inventory and sender-risk reconciliation path that a real pilot uses, instead of relying on inline sender accounts. Acceptance: npm run demo:manual-pilot registers stored sender accounts, creates the campaign from senderPool ids without inline senderAccounts, records a manual restricted event, shows senderWarnings=1, shows the restricted sender in cooldown with a restriction risk event, and keeps final proof review evidence-ready because another sender remains healthy. Validation: manualPilotRehearsal test covers sender warning/cooldown proof, demo output includes the sender-risk summary, docs/status mention the stronger rehearsal, and test/typecheck/build/demo/MMDX preflight pass.
close_reason: Manual pilot rehearsal now registers managed senders, creates campaigns from stored sender ids, records restricted manual evidence into sender cooldown, exposes senderWarnings=1 and sender-risk summary in demo output, and updates docs/status proof. Verified with targeted/manual server tests, full npm test, typecheck, build, service smoke, manual pilot demo, MMDX preflight, and git diff --check.

title: Count operator triage in proof packs
description: Acceptance: pilot proof metrics and Markdown report include operator skipped and operator blocked candidates from the approval workbench, so stored skip/block workbench decisions are visible in renewal evidence. Validation: proof-pack tests cover skipped/blocked workbench candidates; server execution test with skipped candidate asserts proof metrics; docs/status describe operator triage in proof semantics.
close_reason: Proof packs now report explicit operator skipped/blocked workbench evidence; tests, docs, MMDX preflight, and live API smoke passed.

title: Harden pilot-intake webhook SSRF guard + fix IPv6 multicast gap
description: CRAP sweep: scripts/validate-pilot-intake.ts::isBlockedIpHost was CRAP 160 (CC 32, cov 0.50) and parsePilotIntakeArgs CRAP 72 (cov 0.00). Added 38 load-bearing tests driving the webhook destination guard (all blocked IPv4/IPv6 ranges, allowlist/wildcard, protocol, localhost) and the CLI arg parser through the exported public API. Found and fixed a real bug: the proof-gate validator did not block IPv6 multicast (ff00::/8) while the runtime server guard did - the intake gate could clear a callback the server rejects. Added regression test. isBlockedIpHost 160->32 (cov 1.00), parsePilotIntakeArgs 72->~0. TS scope FINAL_SCORE 160->63.53 (residual = buildServer framework wiring, intentionally not chased).
close_reason: Completed: fix landed, 38 tests added and green, typecheck clean, FINAL_SCORE 160->63.53

title: Make public evidence docs citation-grade
description: Outcome: evaluator-facing docs distinguish verified current sources from operator-provided bounty interpretation and weak/inaccessible claims. Acceptance: docs/SOURCE_EVIDENCE.md records current retrieval results for Graphed Notion, Meta Instagram Messaging, Meta private replies, Postman Meta send API, Unipile, Waveloop, Lyncly, and weak/inaccessible sources; docs/BUILD_VS_CLONE.md and docs/BOUNTY_REQUIREMENTS.md only make claims supported by SOURCE_EVIDENCE or explicitly mark assumptions; README/VISION/MARKETING-SURFACE avoid unsupported compliance or market claims. Validation: targeted rg drift checks, MMDX preflight if status changes, npm test or docs-only rationale, br ready/blocked, git diff --check.
close_reason: Hardened evaluator-facing source evidence and softened unsupported public claims.

title: Add managed provider bridge handoff
description: Acceptance: add a credential-free provider bridge command or API-side contract that exports approved send intents to an external managed-delivery endpoint shape and records returned provider outcomes through the existing managed_provider execution path. Include docs/proof/status updates and validation.
close_reason: Added managed-provider bridge fixture and rehearsal, wired it into proof:bounty-local, updated docs/status/proof, and verified full local proof.

title: Add one-command pilot proof demo
description: Acceptance: npm script runs a deterministic local pilot demo without credentials, exercises campaign creation, approval, mock delivery, webhook delivery records, and proof-pack generation, and prints a concise Markdown proof report. Validation: npm run demo:pilot plus existing test/typecheck/build gates.
close_reason: Added npm run demo:pilot deterministic local proof demo exercising campaign, approval, mock delivery, simulated webhook records, and proof-pack markdown; fixed proof metrics to avoid double-counting failed targets.

title: Wire runtime webhook callbacks
description: Outcome: Graphed can receive signed delivery/reply callbacks from normal API flows instead of only polling status. Acceptance: buildServer has an injectable/live outgoing webhook sender, provider event ingestion dispatches a target webhook when campaign settings include webhookUrl, non-simulated executions use the runtime dispatcher, response bodies expose delivery/dead-letter records, tests cover signed dispatch and failure records, docs/status describe live callbacks versus simulation.
close_reason: Runtime webhooks now dispatch from provider events and non-simulated executions, expose dead-letter list/replay, and are covered by server tests and docs.

title: Add optional API key protection
description: Outcome: public service deployments can require an API key while local demos remain default-open. Acceptance: runtime config reads INSCHNEIDERGRAM_API_KEY, buildServer protects non-public API routes when configured, health/openapi/OPTIONS remain public, service smoke runs with auth enabled, README/runbook/domain/status docs name the deployment gate, and tests cover missing/wrong/header/bearer cases.
close_reason: Implemented optional API key protection, runtime wiring, smoke coverage, docs, and status diagram updates.

title: Expose pilot launch readiness report
description: Acceptance: API exposes a campaign readiness report that summarizes whether a pilot is ready, what gates pass/fail, and which external inputs remain before a live Graphed run. It uses current campaign, approval workbench, sender health, and execution proof state. Validation: domain/server tests cover missing workbench, missing approvals, unavailable senders, ready manual execution, and post-reply proof; README/runbook/domain/status docs describe the readiness semantics.
close_reason: Added pilot readiness domain/API report with server/domain tests, docs, MMDX status update, validation, and live smoke.

title: Block execution while manual evidence is pending
description: Outcome: a campaign cannot create another execution while a prior manual execution still has pending operator evidence. Acceptance: readiness status awaiting_manual_evidence causes POST /campaigns/:id/executions to return 409 until the latest manual execution evidence is complete; the conflict message names pending manual evidence; tests cover duplicate manual execution prevention. Validation: targeted server/readiness tests, npm test, typecheck, build, smoke:service, demo:manual-pilot, MMDX preflight, git diff --check.
close_reason: Execution readiness now treats pending manual evidence as a blocking condition for subsequent executions, preventing duplicate manual queues while operator evidence remains unresolved. Added 409 regression coverage and updated docs/status. Verified with targeted server/manual/readiness tests, npm test, typecheck, build, service smoke, manual pilot demo, MMDX preflight, Beads cycle check, and git diff --check.

title: Refresh proof dossier public HEAD provenance
description: Update docs/proof/delivery-path-dry-run.md so the local proof dossier references the current public closeout commit instead of the older proof-packet base, while preserving the external live-pilot and MMDX-auth non-claims. Validate with npm run proof:bounty-local.
close_reason: Refreshed proof dossier provenance to current public closeout commit and updated latest proof-run hashes after npm run proof:bounty-local passed.

title: Expose operator dashboard status API
description: Outcome: Graphed/operator can open one API surface to see what needs action across the pilot instead of stitching readiness, manual queue, sender health, follow-ups, proof packs, and webhook dead letters by hand. Acceptance: GET /operator/dashboard returns generatedAt, campaign counts, sender health, webhook dead-letter count, manual queue counts, urgent action items, and per-campaign readiness/manual/follow-up/proof/source summaries; OpenAPI, README, runbook, domain docs, status MMDX, and service smoke describe/check it; tests cover blocked/approved/manual-evidence/follow-up/dead-letter dashboard states. Validation: targeted server test, npm test, typecheck, build, smoke:service, MMDX preflight, br ready/blocked, git diff --check.
close_reason: Implemented protected operator dashboard API with campaign readiness, manual queue, follow-up, proof, sender health, webhook dead-letter summaries, OpenAPI/docs/status updates, service smoke coverage, and full test/build validation.

title: Add persistent campaign store and idempotency
description: Acceptance: campaigns, targets, provider events, idempotency keys, and suppression records persist across process restarts. Validation: storage tests prove create/get/update/replay behavior and migration command is documented.
close_reason: Implemented JSON-backed campaign persistence with idempotency keys and durable suppression records; tests cover create/get/update/replay, cross-campaign suppression, and API idempotency.

title: Refresh wiki-duel report after public closeout
description: Update the bounty readiness report so it reflects that proof-packet closeout, Beads sync, commit, push, and public remote verification have already happened. Keep remaining blockers scoped to external pilot/MMDX auth evidence.
close_reason: Updated wiki-duel readiness report after public closeout verification.

title: Add bounty evaluator submission packet
description: Outcome: a Graphed/evaluator reader can move from public repo to local proof and pilot kickoff without reconstructing the workflow from scattered docs. Acceptance: add a one-command local bounty proof script, npm wrapper, docs/BOUNTY_SUBMISSION.md with reviewer path, commands, live-pilot ask, and non-claims, README links it, status MMDX/proof docs mention the evaluator packet, no secrets/tokens, and remaining blockers stay external. Validation: local proof command or its component gates pass, MMDX preflight, stale-claim searches, br ready/blocked, git diff --check.
close_reason: Added bounty submission packet, one-command local proof gate, and status/proof docs; proof:bounty-local passes.

title: Prove strict creator provenance in manual rehearsal
description: Outcome: the public manual pilot rehearsal proves live-pilot-style creator intake instead of using plain demo handles. Acceptance: npm run demo:manual-pilot creates profile-object targets with source, fitReason, tags, and profile URLs; sets requireTargetProvenance=true; final readiness/proof reports vettedTargets=2 with no creator-vetting external input; sender-risk cooldown proof still passes; docs/status/proof artifact describe the managed-sender plus strict-provenance rehearsal. Validation: manualPilotRehearsal test, npm test, typecheck, build, smoke:service, demo:manual-pilot, MMDX preflight, git diff --check.
close_reason: Manual pilot rehearsal now uses strict creator-provenance profile targets with source, fitReason, tags, profile URLs, and requireTargetProvenance=true. Demo and tests prove vettedTargets=2, no creator-vetting external input, and the sender-risk proof still passes. Verified with targeted/manual tests, npm test, typecheck, build, service smoke, demo, MMDX preflight, and git diff --check.

title: Harden production runtime and webhook proof security
description: Outcome: a network-exposed deployment cannot accidentally run as an unauthenticated outreach API with forgeable webhooks, webhook callback dispatch is resistant to DNS rebinding between precheck and send, and proof packs include callback delivery outcomes for late provider events. Acceptance: production or non-loopback HOST startup requires strong INSCHNEIDERGRAM_API_KEY and INSCHNEIDERGRAM_WEBHOOK_SECRET; local demos remain usable without secrets on loopback/default local config; outbound webhook dispatch pins prevalidated public DNS answers through the actual HTTP client connection for initial delivery, retry, and replay; late provider-event webhook delivery/dead-letter records are persisted back into the latest proof pack; README/Docker docs reflect required production env. Validation: npm test -- tests/runtimeConfig.test.ts tests/server.test.ts tests/outgoingWebhook.test.ts tests/proofPack.test.ts, npm test, npm run typecheck, npm run build, npm run smoke:service, npm run demo:manual-pilot, MMDX preflight, git diff --check.
close_reason: Production/non-loopback startup now requires strong API and webhook secrets; webhook dispatch passes prevalidated DNS answers into the sender; late provider webhook delivery/dead-letter records refresh the latest execution proof pack; renewal recommendations iterate on warnings/failures/dead letters. Verified targeted tests, npm test, typecheck, build, smoke:service, manual demo, MMDX preflight, git diff --check, and Beads cycle check.

title: Add MMDX status publish handoff
description: Outcome: the public-status blocker is mechanically actionable from the repo instead of depending on remembered local commands. Acceptance: package scripts or repo script can preflight, dry-run, list owned Buildooor MMDX links, start SPAPS login for client_id buildooor, and publish diagrams/inschneidergram-project-status.mmdx to a supplied slug via existing mmdx/SPAPS CLIs without printing tokens. Docs/status/Beads distinguish auth or missing-short-link failures from missing local tooling. Validation at implementation time: preflight and dry-run passed, list/publish were correctly gated by then-current auth state, br graph clean, git diff --check.
notes: 2026-05-30 follow-up: Buildooor auth was refreshed and the stable short link was created/live-verified under inschneidergram-j8b.7, so this handoff remains tooling history rather than an active auth blocker.
close_reason: Added token-safe status:mmdx npm scripts and refreshed public-link blocker evidence.
